ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Ice Limau (calamansi iced lime drink). Closest match is lime juice beverage (FOODON_03301710); calamansi is a citrus hybrid closely related to lime.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Lime juice) used as closest defensible match. Ice Limau is a diluted, sweetened, iced calamansi-lime beverage; no dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for this preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Ice Limau or calamansi lime beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of Ice Limau is approximately 250 ml (250 g), as typically served at Singapore hawker centres and coffee shops.

nutrient_source.title: Ice Limau — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ice Limau (cr_id D09020049). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical Singapore source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ice Limau (calamansi iced lime drink with sugar syrup). GI estimated at 40 (low) based on analogy with diluted fruit juice beverages with modest sugar content from the University of Sydney GI Database. Calamansi juice is a citrus beverage with low sugar (1.24 g/100 g from HPB) and moderate total carbohydrate (7.03 g/100 g). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (40 × 7.03 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 7.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (5 mg/100 g) and low sugar content
hypertension.guidance: Ice Limau is suitable for individuals with hypertension when consumed without added sugar; its negligible sodium (5 mg/100 g) and low calorie content (29 kcal/100 g) do not raise blood pressure, though sweetened hawker versions with added sugar syrup should be requested as 'siu dai' (less sweet) or 'kosong' (no sugar) to avoid excess caloric intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~40), low sugar (1.24 g/100 g), moderate total carbohydrate (7.03 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ice Limau prepared without added sugar syrup is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes given its low glycemic index (~40), low intrinsic sugar (1.24 g/100 g), and glycemic load of approximately 7 per 250 ml serving; patients should specify 'no sugar' when ordering to avoid the sweetened version which would substantially raise postprandial glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat, low sodium, and vitamin C from calamansi lime
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ice Limau is a heart-friendly beverage with negligible saturated fat (0 g/100 g), very low sodium (5 mg/100 g), and calamansi-derived vitamin C which supports endothelial function; the unsweetened version is preferred to avoid excess sugar intake that may adversely affect triglyceride levels in cardiovascular disease patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ice Limau is a popular Singaporean cold beverage made by squeezing calamansi (Citrus microcarpa), a small citrus hybrid native to Southeast Asia, into iced water, typically with a small amount of sugar syrup. It is a staple at Singapore hawker centres, coffee shops (kopitiams), and food courts, commonly ordered alongside meals. The drink is characterised by its refreshing, tart, and mildly sweet flavour profile, with a pale yellow-green colour and a thin, watery consistency. Calamansi provides a distinctive sour citrus note that is sharper than regular lime and more aromatic than lemon. Nutritionally, Ice Limau is a low-calorie beverage (29 kcal/100 g) with minimal protein (0.2 g), negligible fat (0.15 g), and moderate carbohydrate (7.03 g/100 g) primarily from the sugar syrup. The intrinsic sugar content is low at 1.24 g/100 g, and sodium is negligible at 5 mg/100 g. Dietary fibre (0.58 g/100 g) and calcium (11 mg/100 g) are present in small amounts from the calamansi juice. The beverage is suitable for most dietary patterns including vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease when ordered without added sugar. The vitamin C content from calamansi supports immune function and collagen synthesis. It is commonly consumed at breakfast, lunch, and dinner as a refreshing accompaniment to hawker meals.
common_user_queries: How many calories in ice limau?
Is ice limau suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink ice limau if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of ice limau?
How much sugar is in ice limau?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020049), which is the primary canonical source for Singapore food composition. All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this approved source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, documented accordingly.
notes: saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB source — Ice Limau contains no animal fat or tropical oil components. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero as this is a plant-based citrus beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for Ice Limau not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this record. GI value of 40 is an analogous estimate based on diluted citrus fruit juice beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Ice Limau (calamansi iced drink) was found in the approved GI source database. The input description references 'sugar syrup' as an ingredient; the HPB nutrient values reflect a standard preparation. Sweetened versions served at hawker centres may have higher sugar and carbohydrate values than reported here.
